730.reason: [When doPeek(peek) is null, the code increments cur and continues, potentially skipping the current token without parsing it. This is incorrect because it loses the token and may cause the parser to miss necessary syntax components.]

1154.reason: The member variable 'peek' is used to store the start position of the LVal, but it is overwritten by recursive calls in parseUnaryExp, causing incorrect backtracking.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The member variable 'peek' is used to store the start position of the LVal, but it is overwritten by recursive calls in parseUnaryExp, causing incorrect backtracking.

1168.reason: Because 'peek' was modified by parseUnaryExp (called inside parseLVal), this resets 'cur' to the wrong position (e.g., inside the array index expression or at the closing bracket), leading to parsing errors and incorrect token output.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Because 'peek' was modified by parseUnaryExp (called inside parseLVal), this resets 'cur' to the wrong position (e.g., inside the array index expression or at the closing bracket), leading to parsing errors and incorrect token output.

1794.reason: The global variable 'peek' is modified here. If this method is called during the parsing of an expression inside an LVal (e.g., array index), it overwrites the 'peek' value saved in parseStmt, causing parseStmt to reset 'cur' to the wrong position (the closing bracket ']') instead of the start of the LVal.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line overwrites the shared member variable 'peek', which corrupts the backtrack position saved in parseStmt.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The global variable 'peek' is modified here. If this method is called during the parsing of an expression inside an LVal (e.g., array index), it overwrites the 'peek' value saved in parseStmt, causing parseStmt to reset 'cur' to the wrong position (the closing bracket ']') instead of the start of the LVal.
